title: Record: 11L LeakyReLU² + VRL + lzma — val_bpb 1.1229 (3-seed mean)
body:
## Summary

val_bpb = 1.1229 (3-seed mean, std 0.0005) | ~15.89 MB | 8×H100 SXM

### 3-Seed Results (8×H100 80GB SXM, PyTorch 2.9.1+cu128)

| Seed | step_avg | steps | val_bpb | Artifact |
|------|----------|-------|---------|----------|
| 1337 | 87.1ms | 6,889 | 1.1234 | 15,887,926 |
| 42 | 88.0ms | 6,818 | 1.1225 | 15,877,570 |
| 2025 | 87.5ms | 6,857 | 1.1228 | 15,890,566 |
| **Mean** | **87.5ms** | **6,855** | **1.1229 (std 0.0005)** | |

All 3 artifacts under 16,000,000 bytes. All 3 train logs attached.

### Key Innovations

**LeakyReLU(0.5)²**: One-line activation swap preserving negative gradient flow through MLP. ~-0.002 BPB vs standard relu². Credit: PR #493 @parinzee, PR #518 @sofiabod.

**Value Residual Learning (VRL)**: Layer 0's V output blended into all subsequent attention layers via learned sigmoid gates. Combats attention concentration (ResFormer, arXiv:2410.17897). +10 scalar params. Credit: PR #569 @gowtham0992.

**lzma compression**: Stdlib replacement for zstd-22, compresses 2-5% tighter on quantized weights. Recovers ~300-500KB headroom, enabling full MLP 3× + BigramHash 2048 under 16MB without capacity cuts. No external dependencies.

### Architecture

PR #414 base + LeakyReLU² + VRL + lzma:

| Component | Details |
|-----------|---------|
| Layers | 11L, 512d, 8H/4KV (GQA), U-Net skips (5 enc, 6 dec) |
| MLP | 3× expansion (1536), **LeakyReLU(0.5)²** activation |
| Attention | XSA4, Partial RoPE 16/64, LN Scale 1/√(i+1), **VRL** |
| Embeddings | BigramHash(2048), VE128 (layers 9-10), SmearGate |
| Training | EMA(0.997) + Tight SWA, Late QAT (STE@0.15), OrthoInit |
| Optimizer | Muon WD=0.04, warmdown=3500, batch=786K tokens |
| Quantization | GPTQ-lite int6 + **lzma** (preset=6) |
| Attention kernel | FlashAttention 3 (Hopper native) |

### Credits

- Base model: PR #414 by @signalrush
- LeakyReLU²: PR #493 by @parinzee, PR #518 by @sofiabod
- VRL: ResFormer (arXiv:2410.17897), PR #569 by @gowtham0992
- XSA: PR #287 by @jfprincz
- Competition infrastructure: OpenAI, RunPod

## Test plan
- [x] Seed 1337: 1.1234 bpb, 15.89MB valid
- [x] Seed 42: 1.1225 bpb, 15.88MB valid
- [x] Seed 2025: 1.1228 bpb, 15.89MB valid
- [x] 3-seed mean: 1.1229, std 0.0005
- [x] All 3 train logs attached
- [x] All artifacts under 16,000,000 bytes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

comments.body:
These log lines say "int8+zlib" but the artifact being written is int6 plus the configured compressor (currently lzma). This is misleading when inspecting logs and comparing submission sizes; update the message(s) to reflect the actual quantization/compressor being used.
```suggestion

```

This log label references an "int8+zlib" roundtrip, but this path is evaluating the int6+lzma artifact. Rename the log label so it matches what was actually loaded/decompressed to avoid confusion when tracking regressions.

This second occurrence also labels the result as an "int8+zlib" roundtrip even though it’s still evaluating the int6+lzma artifact. Keeping these labels consistent with the actual codec will make log parsing and comparisons more reliable.

`late_k_layers` is computed but never used, which makes the quantization path harder to follow. Remove it or wire it into the intended late-layer quantization behavior.
```suggestion

```

The reproduction command points to the 2026-03-23_Reproduce414_LegalTTT script rather than this record’s train_gpt.py, so following the README won’t reproduce the stated result. Update the path to the correct 2026-03-24_LeakyReLU2_VRL_LZMA/train_gpt.py (or explain why the older script is intended).
```suggestion
records/track_10min_16mb/2026-03-24_LeakyReLU2_VRL_LZMA/train_gpt.py
```

This submission metadata leaves val_bpb/bytes_total/bytes_code as null. If any tooling expects numeric values (as in other records), this will break parsing/aggregation. Consider either populating these fields with the actual numbers or omitting this folder/metadata until the run is complete.
```suggestion
    "val_bpb": 0.0,
    "bytes_total": 0,
    "bytes_code": 0,
```

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Populate numeric score fields in track submission metadata**

This `submission.json` is in the `track_10min_16mb` records tree but leaves `val_bpb`, `bytes_total`, and `bytes_code` as `null`; downstream tooling that ranks or validates submissions using numeric comparisons/arithmetic on these fields will either fail or skip this entry. Since the repo’s submission contract requires `val_bpb` and related metadata for acceptance, these fields should be concrete numbers (or the folder should be moved out of record submissions).
